findings.summary: repo setting forbade GITHUB_TOKEN from creating PRs — the standing release PR would 422 on first run; fixed: can_approve_pull_request_reviews flipped to true (default token permissions stay read)

include-component-in-tag defaults true → tags would be vfkb-v0.2.0, breaking ADR-0056's vX.Y.Z contract and ADR-0057's tag trigger; fixed: include-component-in-tag false, deep-compare verified no other config key changed

GITHUB_TOKEN-created resources trigger no workflows: the release PR needs a human close/reopen for required checks, and the tag/release will not fire a downstream publish workflow — documented in PR comment 4945022179; resolution owned by ADR-0057's implementing PR (workflow_dispatch or App/PAT token; note the suppression is on the creation side, so a release:published trigger alone cannot work)
permissions omitted issues:write required by the pinned action's contract for labeling — added; no publish-capable permission crept in

the ADR's chore-only 0.1.1 canary is impossible (chore is non-releasable) and the first cycle will propose 0.2.0 sweeping full unreleased history — documented as the expected first observable; Release-As is the escape hatch
no repository == guard; forks pushing to their own main get a harmless red run on their side
graphify-out/graph.json is stale (docs-side nodes missing for recent ADRs/RFCs) — repo hygiene outside this PR
